meta.executive_summary: Fixture blueprint exercising the domain-invariant + product-model sections.

key_decisions.title: Balances are recomputed from an append-only ledger

decision_chain.root: Provide metered billing with correct accounting.

domain_invariants.invariant: An account's spendable balance never goes negative — debits are checked against the remaining balance and rejected past zero.
domain_invariants.failure_mode: A customer spends value they never funded; the ledger and the charged total diverge.

domain_invariants.invariant: Event ingestion is at-most-once per idempotency key; re-delivering the same event must not double-count usage.
domain_invariants.failure_mode: Re-delivered events double-count usage and over-bill the customer.

product_model.summary: Illustrative: a metered-billing service where append-only events draw down account balances, a ledger gates spend, and issued charge records are immutable.

entities.name: AccountBalance
entities.lifecycle: created on account open; recomputed per debit

derived_invariants.invariant: A credit cannot be retroactively reduced below the amount already spent against it.
derived_invariants.failure_mode: Forces a negative historical balance; recompute silently corrupts ledger vs balance.

unenforced_invariants.expected_law: An issued charge record's total equals the sum of its line amounts.
unenforced_invariants.why_expected: Standard double-entry conservation; the product issues charge records customers are billed against.
unenforced_invariants.risk: A line-mutation path that skips total recompute drifts the charged amount from the line sum, undetected.
